missing_considerations: Detailed characterization of the joint swelling (location, type, duration, associated pain).
Quantified range of motion measurements.
Response to any interventions attempted (medications, physical therapy).
Laboratory investigations: Complete blood count, uric acid level, inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP), and joint fluid analysis (if indicated) are crucial to rule out septic arthritis and gout.
Imaging studies: X-rays or MRI may be necessary to assess the extent of the joint damage and rule out other conditions.
